summary: Evidence indicates a compromise involving C2 communication and staging of suspicious binaries. Masquerading of svchost.exe in a non-standard directory is observed, consistent with persistence mechanisms.
null_hypothesis: The system is assumed benign. However, the presence of svchost.exe in c:\windows\system32\dllhost\ and connections to known malicious IPs (207.58.245.179) contradicts the null hypothesis.

artifacts.name: Masquerading Binary
artifacts.description: System binary found in non-standard directory, consistent with masquerading.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Check MFT, Prefetch, and Shimcache for execution evidence.

artifacts.name: C2 Server
artifacts.description: Remote server associated with malicious payload delivery and C2 communication.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Review firewall and proxy logs for connection duration and data volume.

artifacts.name: Staged Executable
artifacts.description: Executable found in user temp directory, consistent with staging.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Check Shimcache and Amcache for execution status.

artifacts.name: Malicious Payload
artifacts.description: URL associated with malicious payload delivery.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Execution status unverified — Prefetch/Shimcache/Amcache/Event Logs not examined for this artifact.
